message.content:
Test file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-20_23-32-48_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: cursed sword returns 165 G
Expected: 165 (100 base + 50 curse + 10 first-insurance + 5 fee)
Current error: 115 vs 165
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-20_23-32-48_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts

Important interpretation: first-insurance is 10% of each item's UNMODIFIED base premium (not the curse-adjusted total). The integration example confirms: 100 base + 50 curse + 10 first-insurance = 160 + 5 fee = 165.

Need to track item-specific surcharges. For each item:
- itemBase = BASE_PREMIUM_BY_TYPE[type] (or 60/3 = 20 for component in block)
- if item.cursed → itemBase += 50% surcharge applied to base
- item contributes (itemBase + curseSurcharge) to policy base premium
- first-insurance for the item = 10% of unmodified base premium

Refactor approach: compute per-item contribution + per-item firstInsurance.

For now keep components grouped (block uses 60 group total).

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the current implementation and make minimal changes to pass the failing test.
Now I understand the structure. I need to track per-item base premium and apply cursed surcharge per item. Let me make the minimal change.

content.text:
All 12 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Changed grouping to track items (not just count), then for non-block groups iterate items and apply cursed +50% surcharge per item, while first-insurance is computed from each item's unmodified base premium.
**Result**: All tests now pass (12 passing, 43 todo)
**Approach**: Minimal restructure — kept the block-discount path identical for components (groups of 3), and only added per-item iteration for non-block items so cursed surcharge can be applied to individual item bases. First-insurance accumulates from the unmodified base (or 60 for the block group), matching the test's "10% of unmodified base premium" interpretation. No other modifiers (loyalty, follow-up, high-enchantment) added since current tests do not demand them.

Relevant files:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-20_23-32-48_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts
